title: [FIRRTL] Update the memory module name
body:
This commit adds a name for the memory module.
Use the `name` attribute on `firrtl.mem` to create the name.
 The `name` attribute on `firrtl.mem` is used for the instance name after
 lowering in `LowerToHW`. Add another attribute `modName` to assign a module
 name. The module name must be unique in the entire circuit, hence its a
 global process and can only be done during a global pass.
 
An issue is that, the memory name is being generated during metadata emission.
But the `firrtl.mem` is being lowered to the extern module after metadata
 emission, during `LowerToHW`.

This commit updates the `CreateSifiveMetadata` pass, to iterate over all memory
 nodes, and create unique memory module names.

If the metadata emission pass does not update the `modName` then, use the
 memory properties to generate a memory name.
This commit fixes a bug with the reverted commit https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/2490
The previous commit was not merging memories with the same properties.
